MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
Off Calendar Jun-21-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Paul Wilkins, Esq., 650-594-4200, appearing for Robert Bertucci, Plaintiff and moving party.

Berko, 415-771-6174, (Card provided incomplete) appearing for Defendant/opposing party.

ARGUED. Mr. Berko stated that he filed a challenge to Judge Haines but it was rejected by the clerk. The Court takes a brief recess to research the challenge. For the record, the court stated that Mr. Berko filed a document titled proof of service and in that body of that document there was a 170.6 challenge to Judge Haines. The clerk rejected this document with the explanation that the documents do not match and that if it were to be refiled after June 14, 2019 the challenge would be untimely. Mr. Berko refiled the corrected challenge on June 18, 2019. The Court ruled that it was untimely.

Mr. Berko stated that he contested to the tentative ruling and provided case background.
The Court and counsel discuss the ruling made in San Mateo County Superior Court. The Court stated that it was considering staying the entry of judgment pending appeal. Mr. Wilkins reported that the trial is in September and that a stay may complicate things.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF ROBERT BERTUCCI MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS is GRANTED. Cross-Complainant has no standing to maintain causes of action for conversion and FC 1101 at this time. Judge Charles F. Haines, Clerk: N. Wong, Not reported (D501)
